candidates.why: Clearly labeled top-line revenue; matches REVT definition and has consistent positive sales figures.
Represents financial income, not operating revenue; excluded in favor of operating revenue line.
Interest income, part of financial income, not core operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Earnings measure, not revenue.
Other/non-core income, not total revenue.
Other operating income, supplementary to revenue, not the main sales line.
Net operating result, not top-line revenue.

candidates.why: Technical balancing item, not revenue.
Financial income, not sales.
Balancing line for financial income, not revenue.
Balancing item for pretax income, not revenue.
Tax expense, not revenue.
Tax-related, not revenue.
Total taxes, not revenue.

candidates.why: Balancing item tied to net income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Net Income After Extraordinary Items applicable to Common
candidates.why: Balancing item tied to net income, not revenue.
Net income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Diluted Net Income excluding Extra Items applicable to Common - (Company Level)
candidates.why: Adjusted net income, not revenue.
Non-recurring item, not ongoing revenue.

candidates.why: Per-share earnings, not revenue.

candidates.why: Per-share earnings, not revenue.

candidates.why: Allocated net income, not revenue.

candidates.why: Allocated diluted net income, not revenue.

variables.notes: Total Revenue is the explicit and only true top-line sales figure; no alternative revenue label is needed as a backup.

candidates.why: Direct production/materials cost; primary COGS component with substantial values.
Label explicitly refers to cost of goods; likely an alternative or complementary COGS line though currently all shown as nan.
Primarily operating personnel costs; more SG&A-like than direct COGS for this mapping.
Could include outsourced production or services, but is too generic and appears unused (all nan).
Broad operating overhead; better treated as SG&A than COGS.
External overhead-type expenses; more SG&A-like.
Maintenance expense; overhead, not direct COGS.
Marketing expense; SG&A, not COGS.
IP-related overhead; SG&A, not COGS.
Miscellaneous overhead; SG&A-type.
Net operating result after revenues and expenses; not an expense component.
Financing cost, not operating COGS.
Financing-related; not COGS.
Financing-related; not COGS.
Non-recurring item; should not be treated as ongoing COGS.
Inventory component on balance sheet, not period COGS expense.

variables.notes: Both 'Cost of goods' and 'Cost of materials' are direct production cost lines and together best represent COGS; other candidates are either overhead (SG&A) or financing-related.

candidates.why: Personnel expenses for operations; typical SG&A component (overhead salaries).
Broad operating overhead bucket; classic SG&A line excluding COGS, D&A, and interest.
Equity-based compensation; explicitly part of SG&A-type overhead and should be included as an SG&A component.

variables.notes: These three rows capture the main SG&A overhead components; no totals or balancing items are included, and D&A/impairments are excluded.

candidates.why: Represents capitalized R&D on the balance sheet, not a period expense line.
Accumulated amortization of capitalized R&D, a balance sheet contra-asset, not current R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Own product development, Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing/net asset figure for capitalized R&D, not an income statement expense.

variables.notes: No explicit R&D expense line appears on the Income Statement; available candidates are balance sheet capitalization/amortization items, which do not represent current-period R&D expense for PROF.

candidates.why: Explicit interest expense related to lease liabilities; a valid component of interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Generic interest expense line (net of capitalized amounts); ideal primary XINT line if populated.
Represents minority shareholders’ share of profit, not interest expense; should not be used for XINT.
Generic interest expense line; conceptually the best overall match for XINT even if current preview values are nan.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; valid backup interest expense source.
Dividends, not interest expense.
Equity line for minority interests, not interest expense.
Equity line for minority interests, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Priority is given to the most generic interest expense lines, with lease-specific interest as fallbacks; non-controlling-interest-related lines are excluded as they are not financing costs.

candidates.why: Represents total shareholders’ equity; closest available proxy for book equity attributable to owners given lack of a separate parent-equity line.
Duplicate total equity line (different capitalization); useful as an alternative label across periods.
Technical balancing line, not the primary equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing item for the accounting equation, not an equity subtotal.
Total assets/equity+liabilities; not pure equity.

variables.notes: No separate ‘equity attributable to owners’ line exists, so Total Equity/Total equity are used as book equity. To avoid double counting, MIB is set to zero (no separate owner-equity line to net out non-controlling interests).

candidates.why: Represents income attributable to non-controlling interests, not the balance sheet equity amount; would normally be the best income-statement-based MIB, but BE uses Total Equity here.
Dividends paid to NCI, not the standing minority equity balance.
Balance sheet minority interest line; conceptually correct for MIB if BE excluded NCI from equity.
Alternate label for balance sheet minority interest; also conceptually correct for MIB if BE were parent-only equity.
Income-statement share of profit to minorities, not the equity balance; not needed given BE choice.

variables.notes: Because BE is mapped to Total Equity (which likely already includes any non-controlling interests and there is no separate parent-only equity line), MIB is set to zero here to avoid potential double counting in BE + MIB.
